next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 14

"Path forks," Vel said. "South-southeast. Water first."

The lower road bent into a settlement that had made a long habit of not collapsing all at once. Kem saw the lean before he saw the people. Three houses on the east lip listed toward a split in the ground, one already down in a heap of stone, broken joists, and roof tile. The west side held the well, the cookfires, the chickens, the people who had learned where not to stand. Between the two sat a retaining wall of quarried blocks laid years ago by somebody who had trusted the seam under them more than the seam had deserved.

Dren squinted at the well. "Good."

That was all. Not good settlement, not good rest, not even good ground. Just water. His bad forearm was wrapped tight against his chest and still looked heavier than the rest of him.

They walked in under a net of watching eyes. In the Margins, strangers meant trade, trouble, or bodies. Usually more than one. A woman with two sacks of grain slung over one shoulder watched Vel first, then Kem, then the distance between them. An old man by the wall lifted three boards, set down two, reconsidered the third, and went on pretending he was not listening. Two boys argued over whose turn it was to carry broken tile and fell silent when they noticed Kem's boots.

Vel headed for the well without asking permission. Dren veered with her. Kem followed because there was nowhere else to go that would not read as performance.

At the curb, a woman with a shaved scalp and flour on her forearms kept winding the bucket rope as if newcomers were no more surprising than...
open_hooks_to_carry: Kem's emergency road-collapse spend (12 levels) and incidentals (7 levels) dropping him from 50 to 31 — Vel's starting number
The cascade mechanism explained: co-spending in proximity stabilizes the threshold; high-to-zero descent amplifies the effect
Vel's manipulation exposed: she needed a high-level spender willing to burn down; Thessa's Ch 10 seed pays off — she's been looking for years
Kem's cold confrontation enhanced with Margins awareness: 'You recruited me as a component. That's what they do here. You replicated the system you claim to oppose.'
Vel's inability to answer because the accusation is accurate — the delay is its own answer
The alliance fractured but intact — they cannot survive alone at these levels
Kem's House signet: taken off his hand, considered for discarding, returned to his pocket

payoffs_to_land.description: Vel's strategic use of Kem is fully exposed — her level-assessment in Ch 3, her spending guidance, her concealment of requirements were all calculated
Vel's private planning about the crossing is revealed to have always included a high-level co-spender as a necessary component
Thessa's Ch 10 revelation pays off: Vel has been searching for a high-level spender for years, making the manipulation premeditated, not opportunistic
The Margins' moral reality contaminates Kem's confrontation — his accusation that Vel replicated the Hoarded's logic of using people gains force from the exploited landscape around them

prev_tail_excerpt:
"Yeah."

"You don't have to make it look polite."

"I wasn't."

He walked three more paces before speaking again. "If something ahead needs spending, I'm not asking permission first."

"All right."

He glanced at her, not satisfied. "No. I mean it. You tell me what the road costs. Distance. Water. What I burn if I use something. You don't do the deciding in your head and hand me the clean version after."

That landed close enough to bone that Vel felt it in her teeth. "Fine," she said. "I give you the number. You spend or you don't."

"Good."

"Don't mistake that for trust."

"I don't."

He dropped back again, as if that settled what could be settled.

The notices in her pack rasped against the journal buckle every time she moved. Once the corner of the Productivity Tier notice caught in the leather and tugged at her shoulder until she jerked the strap straight.

She had not told him to stop. She had done worse. She had started giving him the numbers.

Three days south the basin waited. Maren had marked it. Fallow had named two others, Sera and Hoster. When the flash came back now, it brought white stone, hard wind, and the dark wet line creeping through Kem's sleeve.

She walked south, and every mile pressed harder.
state_deltas_required: The discovery that Vel engineered the alliance around the cascade mechanism; Kem's betrayal contaminated by the Margins' moral weight
The cascade mechanism revealed; the alliance cracks over Vel's manipulation, now understood through the Margins' lens as systemic rather than just personal; Kem passes through Vel's starting level
